english: with sleeves Cs.; dpuṅ-pa-bčád (dpung pa bcad) the part of a woman's dress covering the chest Zam.; Sch.: dpuṅ-bčad-rás (dpung bcad ras).

Ld. *spe* (*spe*), 1. pattern, model, dé-la dpe (de la dpe) Glr., or de dper byás-nas (de dper byas nas) Zam., taking this for a pattern; rgyá-yul-nas rtsis-kyi dpe blaṅs (rgya yul nas rtsis kyi dpe blangs) it was from China that mathematics were learned Glr.; ...pai dpe mi _dug (... pa'i dpe mi 'dug) there are no patterns for... Glr.; dpe či ltar (dpe ci ltar) with what to be compared? according to what analogy? Thgy.; similitude, parable, example, mt'ún-pai dpe (mthun pa'i dpe) an example that may be followed, a good example; bzlóg-pai dpe (bzlog pa'i dpe) an example to the contrary, a warning example Thgy.; *pe záṅ-po* (*pe zang po*), and ném-pe pe* (*nem pe pe*), as well as *yár-la* (*yar la*) and már-la žág-pe-pe (*mar la zhag pe pe*), or mar-pe* (*mar pe*) C. id.; dpe stón-pa (dpe ston pa) to teach or to prove by examples; hence the participle, used substantively, serves as an epithet of the Sautrantikas, Was. (112); dpe bšád-pa (dpe bshad pa), dpe bžág-pa = dpe stón-pa (dpe bzhag pa = dpe ston pa); dper rjód-pa (dper rjod pa) to set up for a parable or comparison; dpér-na (dper na), in later times also dpé-ni (dpe ni), dpe byéd-na (dpe byed na) Mil., *pe gyáb-na* (*pe gyab na*) W., 1. (in order) to quote an example, by way of a comparison, just as if, followed by bžin-du (bzhin du) or ltar (ltar), very frq.; 2. like our 'for instance', e.g., before enumerations, where in the older writings gen. _di-lta-ste ('di lta ste) is used: dper _ós-pa (dper 'os pa) Cs. what may be compared, dper mi _ós-pa (dper mi 'os pa) not to be compared; occasionally also: worthy or not worthy of imitation; k'á-dpe (kha dpe), ytám-dpe (gtam dpe) proverb, adage Cs.; _drá-dpe ('dra dpe) allegory, parable S.g.; má-dpe (ma dpe) W., Ld. *má-spe* (*ma spe*), Lh. *már-pe* (*mar pe*), pattern, (writing-) copy (cf. also má-dpe (ma dpe) and bú-dpe (bu dpe) below). — 2. symmetry, harmony, beauty, (in certain phrases). — 3. book, Krims brgyad-kyi dpe (khrims brgyad kyi dpe) the book of the eight commandments Dzl.; ká-dpe (ka dpe), ka-k'ái dpe (ka kha'i dpe) a b c-book, primer; p'yág-dpe (phyag dpe) resp. for dpe (dpe), if used by a Lama (cf. p'yag-mk'ár (phyag mkhar)); má-dpe (ma dpe), bú-dpe (bu dpe) original and copy of a book Cs.; yig-nág dpe (yig nag dpe) a real book, not of a fig. meaning, as the book of nature, Mil.; dpe rtsóm-pa (dpe rtsom pa) to write, to compose, bšú-ba (bshu ba) to copy a book; _dógs-pa ('dogs pa), _tsém-pa ('tsem pa) to bind, to stitch a book. Comp. dpé-ka (dpe ka) little book, vulgo. -- dpe-k'áṅ (dpe khang) library; bookseller's shop. -- dpe-k'ri (dpe khri) a table to put books on, book-stand. -- dpe-mkyúd (dpe mkhyud), k'yud (khyud) Cs. v. mk'yúd-pa (mkhyud pa). -- dpe-mgó (dpe mgo), dpe-mjúg (dpe mjug) beginning, end, of a book. -- dpe-sgám (dpe sgam) chest for books, book-case. -- dpé-ča (dpe cha) not frq. in B., but vulgo the common word for book. -- *dpé-ča p'é-če* (*dpe cha phe che*), *túm-če* (*tum che*) W. to open, to close a book; v. btúm-pa (btum pa). -- dpe-rjód (dpe rjod) v. dper (dper). -- dpe-t'ó (dpe tho) list of books. -- dpe-byád (dpe byad) proportion, symmetry, beauty, dpe-byád bzáṅ-po brgyád-ču (dpe byad bzang po brgyad cu) the eighty physical perfections of Buddha. -- dpe-byád-čan (dpe byad can) well-proportioned. -- dpé-tsoṅ-pa (dpe tshong pa) bookseller -- dpe-šúbs (dpe shubs) case or covering for a book. -- dpe-bšús (dpe bshus) copy of a book. -- dper (dper) v. 1. -- dpe-brjód (dpe brjod) 1. example, comparison, dpe-brjód byéd-pa (dpe brjod byed pa) to compare, to cite an example Cs.; dpe-brjód rtógs-pa (dpe brjod rtogs pa) Gram. id.(?). 2. paradigm, example Gram. —

(?), *(s)pé-ra* (*spe ra*) W., speech, for gtam (gtam); *(s)pé-ra zér-če(s)* (*spe ra zer ces*), *táṅ-če(s)* (*tang ces*), to speak, to talk; *í-zug (s)pé-ra ma taṅ* (*i zug spe ra ma tang*) do not say so! *(s)pé-ra zér-če(s) med-k'an soṅ* (*spe ra zer ces med khan song*) he became speechless (with terror etc.).

pf. dpags (dpags), fut. dpag (dpag), 1. to measure, to proportion, to fix, gtóṅ-tsul če-čúṅ-la (gtong tshul che chung la) (to proportion) the dose to the size Lt.; ...kyi tšód-las (... kyi tshod las) after the measure of ..., Lt.: nad-stóbs-la (nad stobs la) according to the violence of the disease Lt.; dpag (tu) méd (-pa) (dpag (tu) med (-pa)), less frq. dpag-brál (dpag bral), dpag-yás (dpag yas), immensely large, very much; t'ugs dpag-méd (thugs dpag med) infinite grace, mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) to show Dzl. — 2. to outweigh, to counterbalance, loṅs-spyód t'ams-čád-kyis mi dpóg-pa (longs spyod thams cad kyis mi dpog pa) not to be counterbalanced by all the wealth ... Tar. — 3. to weigh, to judge, to prove, rjés-su dpóg-pa (rjes su dpog pa) to examine Tar.; rjes-dpág (rjes dpag) Zam. inference, conclusion.
master, lord, over men (generally): (cf. bdág-po (bdag po) owner) master, over working-men, overseer, foreman, leader, grá-pai dpon-po (gra pa'i dpon po), director, =
